name: Mac Control
description: Native macOS control: read any app's UI tree, click, type, scroll, take screenshots, manage windows, and read/write the clipboard. 13 tools via Apple's accessibility APIs.

setupSteps.title: Why permission is required
setupSteps.instructions: macOS sandboxes apps that read or control other apps' UIs. The Swift binary that powers Mac Control (vodou-ax) needs Accessibility permission to read UI trees and send clicks/keys to other apps. This is the same permission Raycast, Rectangle, BetterTouchTool, etc. ask for. Without it, every tool returns the system error kAXErrorAPIDisabled.

setupSteps.title: Grant Accessibility permission
setupSteps.instructions: Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility. Click the + button, navigate to your Vodou install dir → MCP-servers/vodou-mac-control/bin/, and add vodou-ax-arm64 (Apple Silicon) or vodou-ax-x86_64 (Intel). Toggle it ON.
setupSteps.gotcha: If the binary doesn't appear when you click +, drag it directly from Finder into the Accessibility list. The first attempt to use any tool also surfaces the permission prompt — you can grant it then.

setupSteps.title: (Optional) Grant Screen Recording for screenshots
setupSteps.instructions: If you want to use the screenshot tool, grant Screen Recording permission in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Screen Recording. Add the same vodou-ax-* binary. Without this, Mac Control still works — only the screenshot tool fails.

setupSteps.title: Add server
setupSteps.instructions: Click Add server below. Vodou runs vodou-core connect vodou-mac-control which spawns the bundled stdio launcher. The first tool call uses the Accessibility permission you granted above.
setupSteps.gotcha: If Add server fails with kAXErrorAPIDisabled on the first tool, the permission isn't active yet. Toggle it OFF and back ON in System Settings — macOS sometimes caches the deny. Restart the gateway with ./start-vodou-services.sh if needed.

setupSteps.title: Enable in Tools
setupSteps.instructions: Open Capabilities → MCP Servers and turn vodou-mac-control on. You get 13 tools: traverse, click_element_and_traverse, type_and_traverse, press_key_and_traverse, scroll_and_traverse, open_and_traverse, screenshot, list_windows, manage_window, clipboard_read, clipboard_write, plus two utility tools. Try saying 'list my open windows' or 'take a screenshot' in chat.

setupSteps.title: Verify it works
setupSteps.instructions: From the chat, ask: 'what windows are open?'. The list_windows tool runs and you should see your active apps and window titles. If it errors, re-check the Accessibility toggle in System Settings.
setupSteps.gotcha: Every action returns the full UI tree + a diff. Responses can be large — that's intentional, the AI uses the tree to plan the next click. Don't be alarmed by long tool outputs.
